sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - DAUPHINE de Saint-Bonnet (-1287, bur Mâcon Frères Mineurs).

Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - DAUPHINE de Saint-Bonnet, widow of GUY de Damas Seigneur de Couzan, Vicomte de Chalon, daughter of JOSSERAND Seigneur de Saint-Bonnet & his wife Peytavine --- (-1287, bur Mâcon Frères Mineurs).

zh.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau是Josserand IV de Lavieu和Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois的女兒，於1210年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易拉維額。她於1242年與Guy Ier de Dalmas結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1287年之後在法國布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易沙吉阿布易爾，埋葬在法國布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易沙吉阿布易爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau是Josserand IV de Lavieu和Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois的女儿，于1210年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易拉維額。她于1242年与Guy Ier de Dalmas结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1287年之后在法国布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易沙吉阿布易爾，埋葬在法国布格-福朗-可特艾薩o-吉洛易沙吉阿布易爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lavieu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French   Ledoux   ‘the gentle, the sweet’.

variants.name: Ledoux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Pierre, Gabrielle, Lucien, Camille, Colette, Donat, Normand, Adrien, Aime.
French:

nickname for a gentle-natured man, from the adjective doux ‘sweet, mild-mannered, gentle’ (from Latin dulcis), with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Ladeau , Ladieu , Ladoux , Ladue , and Sweet .

nameMeanings.name: Dauphine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Aurelien.
French (Dauphiné): habitational name from the Dauphiné region of southeastern France (see Dauphin ).

variants.name: Dauphinais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Normand, Adelard, Andre, Armand, Gilles, Henri, Lucien.
Altered form of French Dauphiné (see Dauphine ). Compare Duffany , Duffiney , and Duffney .

variants.name: Dauphin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Andre, Armand, Emile, Antoine, Benoit, Carmelle, Fernand, Georges, Jean Claude, Laure, Luckner, Maryse.

French and Haitian: from the French personal name Dauphin, from Latin Delphinus, derived from Greek delphis ‘dolphin’. This name was borne by a 4th-century Christian saint who was bishop of Bordeaux, and from the early 12th century it was in use as a hereditary personal name in the family of the counts of Albon, so that it soon came to be used as a title and led to their territory (capital Grenoble) being known as the Dauphiné. When it became part of the Kingdom of France in 1349, the title of dauphin thereafter denoted the heir-apparent to the throne, and it is possible that in some cases this is the origin of the surname, either denoting a member of the Dauphin's household or applied as a nickname in the sense of ‘prince’.

variants.name: Dauphinee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Damien.
Altered form of French Dauphiné (see Dauphine ).

en.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau was born in 1210, in Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France as the daughter of Josserand IV de Lavieu and Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. She married Guy Ier de Dalmas in 1242. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1287, in Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, and was buried in Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau est née en 1210, à Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, fille de Josserand IV de Lavieu et Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. Elle a épousé Guy Ier de Dalmas en 1242. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1287, à Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, et a été enterrée à Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

de.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau wurde 1210 in Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich, als Tochter von Josserand IV de Lavieu und Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois geboren. Sie hat 1242 Guy Ier de Dalmas geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1287 in Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich und wurde in Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau è nata il nel 1210 nel Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia come figlia di Josserand IV de Lavieu e Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. Lei sposato Guy Ier de Dalmas nel 1242. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1287, nel Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, e fu sepolta a Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateauは1210年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスロイアーラヴィエウでJosserand IV de LavieuとPeytavine de Poitiers-Valentinoisの娘として生まれた。1242年にGuy Ier de Dalmasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1287年以後にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeサoネ-エト-ロイアーサイント-アンブレウイルで死去し，フランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeサoネ-エト-ロイアーサイント-アンブレウイルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau(은)는 1210년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 로이르 래비외에서 Josserand IV de Lavieu(와)과 Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1242년 Guy Ier de Dalmas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1287년 이후 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 사o니-트-로이르 세인트-아브레우일에서 사망했으며 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 사o니-트-로이르 세인트-아브레우일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau nasceu em 1210, em Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França filha de Josserand IV de Lavieu e Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. Ela casou-se com Guy Ier de Dalmas em 1242. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1287, em Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, e foi sepultada em Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau родилась в 1210 г. в местности лавиеу, лоир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция. Её родителей звали Josserand IV de Lavieu и Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy Ier de Dalmas в 1242 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1287 г. в местности сеинт-амбреуил, саoне-ет-лоир, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция и была похоронена в местности сеинт-амбреуил, саoне-ет-лоир, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Dauphine de Lavieu de Saint-Bonnet-Le-Chateau nació en 1210, en Lavieu, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia como hija de Josserand IV de Lavieu y Peytavine de Poitiers-Valentinois. Se casó con Guy Ier de Dalmas en 1242. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1287, en Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, y fue sepultada en Saint-Ambreuil, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.
